title: Blood and Sand

evidence.note: Published 1922; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — Blood and Sand (1922), full film

editorial: Blood and Sand is a 1922 American silent drama directed by Fred Niblo and produced by Famous Players-Lasky (Paramount), starring Rudolph Valentino, Lila Lee and Nita Naldi. Adapted from Vicente Blasco Ibanez's Spanish novel about a doomed matador, it was one of the top-grossing films of 1922 and among Valentino's most successful vehicles. Published in 1922, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
